LAW AND MOTION 302, THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. PETITIONERS SPRAWLDEF AND DAVID TAM'S HEARING ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS CONTINUED ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION TO SEPTEMBER 14, 2012. THE ACTION IS STAYED FOR SIX MONTHS TO ENABLE THE ACTION TO BE TRANSFERRED TO SOLANO COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT. JUDICIAL ECONOMY REQUIRES THAT THIS ACTION BE HEARD IN SOLANO COUNTY SUPERIOR COURT, WHICH HEARD THE HIGHLY RELATED ISSUES OF CEQA COMPLIANCE AND IS THE COUNTY COURT WHOSE GEOGRAPHIC AREA INCLUDES THE PROPERTY AT ISSUE. IF THE CASE IS NOT TRANSFERRED BY AUGUST 24, 2012, THE PARTIES SHOULD FILE A STATUS STATEMENT NO LATER THAN AUGUST 28 EXPLAINING WHY NOT. PARTIES MAY APPEAR IN THE EX-PARTE CALENDAR FOR ANY CLARIFICATION IT MAY HAVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; NOT REPORTED.

calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Sep-14-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT'S Hearing On Petition For Writ Of MandatE is off calendar. The case was transferred to Solano County. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Mar-14-2012 Continued To Sep-14-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CHRISTIANA TIEDEMANN, SUPERVISING DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. (510) 622-2218 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT;
ATTY. LILY N. CHINN OF BEVERIDGE & DIAMOND, TEL. NO. 262-4000 APPEARING FOR THE REAL PARTY IN INTERES WASTE CONNECTION.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS DISCUSSED. PETITIONERS SPRAWLDEF AND DAVID TAM'S HEARING On Petition For Writ Of Mandate IS Continued on the court's own motion to September 14, 2012. The action is stayed for six months to enable the action to be transferred to Solano County Superior Court. Judicial economy requires that this action be heard in Solano County Superior Court, which heard the highly related issues of CEQA compliance and is the county court whose geographic area includes the property at issue. If the case is not transferred by August 24, 2012, the parties should file a status statement no later than August 28 explaining why not. PARTIES MAY APPEAR IN THE EX-PARTE CALENDAR FOR ANY CLARIFICATION IT MAY HAVE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Not reported. = (302/HEK)

Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate

MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
RESPONDENT SAN FRANCISCO BAY CONSERVATION AND DEVELOPMENT'S Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Mandate is Continued on the court's own motion to March 14, 2012. Judge: Curtis Karnow, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077 =(302/CK)
